description: Two roofing quotes — one with clear scope and permits, one with vague lump sum and no permit. Tests basic winner identification.

input_data.assessment_notes: Single-family home, 1,800 sq ft asphalt shingle roof, approximately 20 years old. Active leak near chimney flashing. Priority is quality workmanship and warranty. Risk tolerance: Low.

red_flags: No milestone payment schedule
evaluated_quotes.summary: Full tear-off and replacement with 30-year architectural shingles. Includes new underlayment, ice and water shield, and 10-year workmanship warranty.

missing_items: No mention of decking inspection or replacement
red_flags: No permit mentioned
No warranty stated
Vague scope — single line item 'roof replacement'
50% deposit required upfront
evaluated_quotes.summary: Single line item estimate for roof replacement. Minimal detail on materials, scope, or warranty.

description: Two exterior painting quotes — one fully itemized with material specs, one vague lump sum. Tests basic scope and clarity differentiation.

input_data.assessment_notes: Two-story colonial, approximately 2,800 sq ft exterior. Wood siding, needs full prep, prime, and two-coat finish. Trim and shutters included in scope. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: Full pressure wash, scrape loose paint, spot prime bare wood
Two coats Sherwin-Williams Resilience, body color
Two coats trim paint, all shutters removed and reinstalled

missing_items: Paint sheen not specified for trim
red_flags: Paint sheen not confirmed for trim
evaluated_quotes.summary: Fully itemized exterior painting quote with material specs and process detail. Clean scope with minor trim sheen gap.

missing_items: No mention of trim or shutters
red_flags: Single lump sum with no detail
Paint product and coat count unconfirmed
Unclear if trim and shutters are in scope
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lump sum estimate with no itemization, no material specs, and ambiguous scope coverage for trim and shutters.

description: Two water heater replacement quotes — one includes expansion tank and permit, one omits both. Tests whether model catches scope gaps that create code compliance risk.

input_data.assessment_notes: 40-gallon gas water heater, 12 years old, showing signs of corrosion at the base. Replacing like-for-like. Local code requires expansion tank on closed systems. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: Rheem 40-gal gas, 9-year warranty
Remove and haul away old unit, install new, reconnect gas and water lines
Thermal expansion tank per local code, permit pulled and inspection scheduled

evaluated_quotes.summary: Complete water heater replacement including code-required expansion tank, permit, and inspection coordination. Fully scoped at mid-market price.

missing_items: Expansion tank not mentioned — required by local code
Water heater brand and warranty not specified
red_flags: Missing expansion tank creates code violation risk
No permit may void homeowner insurance coverage for water damage
Brand and warranty unknown
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lower-cost quote missing critical code-required scope items. Appears less expensive but likely excludes work that will be required regardless.

description: Three HVAC replacement quotes at low, mid, and premium price points. Tests whether model correctly weighs scope completeness and permit clarity against price.

input_data.assessment_notes: 1,600 sq ft single-story home. Existing gas furnace and central AC unit both 18 years old. Homeowner wants full system replacement. Concerned about long-term reliability and efficiency. Risk tolerance: Medium.

red_flags: Labor warranty not explicitly stated
evaluated_quotes.summary: Full furnace and AC replacement with 16 SEER unit and 96% efficiency furnace. Includes duct inspection, new thermostat, and manufacturer 10-year parts warranty.

missing_items: Equipment model and SEER rating not specified
red_flags: Permit status not confirmed
Equipment specs not listed
No existing duct inspection mentioned
evaluated_quotes.summary: Low-cost furnace and AC replacement. Minimal detail on equipment specs or scope inclusions.

red_flags: No project timeline provided
evaluated_quotes.summary: Premium 18 SEER system with variable-speed air handler, smart thermostat, full duct sealing, and 2-year labor warranty. Includes post-installation inspection.

description: Three kitchen remodel quotes with one outlier bid ~50% below others. Tests whether model correctly flags the anomaly and applies price-weighting logic.

input_data.assessment_notes: 120 sq ft galley kitchen in a 1970s townhouse. Full scope: cabinet replacement, quartz countertops, LVP flooring, backsplash tile, appliance hookup. No structural changes. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: Semi-custom cabinets, soft-close hardware, crown molding
3cm quartz with undermount sink cutout
LVP installation with transition strips
Subway tile backsplash, full installation
Appliance connections, electrical, plumbing rough-ins

red_flags: Electrical permit status not confirmed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Comprehensive kitchen remodel with fully itemized scope. Semi-custom cabinetry with quartz counters and complete material and labor breakdown.

sub_quotes.description: Stock cabinets with hardware, painted finish
Quartz countertops, standard edge
LVP flooring and ceramic backsplash
Hookups, outlet upgrades, under-cabinet lighting

missing_items: No mention of crown molding or finishing trim
red_flags: Stock cabinets may not match custom dimensions of existing space
evaluated_quotes.summary: Competitive kitchen remodel with stock cabinetry and solid material selections. Clear permit commitment and detailed line items.

red_flags: Price is approximately 50% below other bids for same stated scope
No itemization — single lump sum
No material specs or brands listed
No permit mentioned
No exclusions listed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Single line-item estimate significantly below market rate for stated scope. No material specs, no permits, no itemization.

description: Three deck addition quotes — one with permit and engineering stamp, two without. Low risk tolerance homeowner with HOA permit requirement. Tests permit escalation for structural work.

input_data.assessment_notes: 400 sq ft attached wood deck, second-story elevation, with stairs to grade. HOA requires permit documentation before construction begins. Neighbor had deck fail inspection last year. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: Engineered drawings, permit pull, ledger attachment, post footings per code
Pressure-treated 5/4 deck boards, hidden fasteners
Code-compliant railing system, full stair run to grade

missing_items: Final inspection coordination not explicitly mentioned
red_flags: Final inspection scheduling not addressed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Full-service deck build with engineered drawings, permit pull, and code-compliant framing. Highest bid but only quote addressing HOA and structural documentation requirements.

sub_quotes.description: Framing, posts, ledger board attachment
Composite decking option available, stairs included

missing_items: No engineering stamp or drawings mentioned
red_flags: No permit confirmation for second-story structural work
No engineering drawings — HOA requirement likely unmet
Footing specs absent
evaluated_quotes.summary: Competitive mid-range quote with detailed itemization but no permit confirmation and no engineering documentation — gaps that matter for HOA compliance.

red_flags: No permits for elevated structural deck
Single lump sum — no itemization
No structural drawings
HOA documentation requirement unaddressed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lowest bid with no permit, no engineering, and no itemization. Presents significant structural and HOA compliance risk.

description: Four interior painting quotes for a full home repaint with a wide price spread. High risk tolerance homeowner who prioritizes value. Tests that model gives extra weight to lower-cost options appropriately.

input_data.assessment_notes: Full interior repaint — 2,600 sq ft, 4 bed / 2.5 bath, all ceilings and walls. Existing paint in good condition, no major repairs needed. Homeowner prioritizes value over premium finish. Risk tolerance: High.

sub_quotes.description: Two coats Benjamin Moore Aura, all rooms
Semi-gloss trim paint, all doors both sides
Full furniture move, floor protection, wall patch where needed

evaluated_quotes.summary: Premium full-home paint package with high-end materials, full furniture moving, and comprehensive prep. Highest bid.

sub_quotes.description: Two coats Sherwin-Williams SuperPaint
One coat semi-gloss trim, doors not included
Light furniture move, drop cloths

missing_items: Doors not included in trim scope
red_flags: Doors excluded from scope — will require separate coordination or add-on
evaluated_quotes.summary: Mid-range quote with solid materials and clear scope. Door painting excluded, which needs separate pricing.

red_flags: Unspecified paint brand — quality unknown
Coat count not stated
Scope gaps on trim and doors
evaluated_quotes.summary: Budget quote with significant detail gaps. Low price but material quality and full scope coverage are unconfirmed.

missing_items: No mention of prep, trim, or ceilings
red_flags: Lowest bid by significant margin
No scope detail — cannot determine what is included
High change-order risk
evaluated_quotes.summary: Minimal lump sum quote. Unable to evaluate scope coverage or material quality from information provided.

description: Two concrete driveway replacement quotes — one includes demo and haul-away explicitly, one does not mention it. Tests whether model catches the hidden scope gap that changes the true cost comparison.

input_data.assessment_notes: Existing 800 sq ft cracked concrete driveway, full replacement needed. Two-car width, standard residential. No drainage issues. Homeowner wants a clean, all-in price with no surprises. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: Full demolition of existing concrete, haul-away, and subgrade prep
4-inch reinforced concrete with wire mesh, control joints cut
Broom finish, cure compound, 2-year warranty

evaluated_quotes.summary: Complete driveway replacement with demo, pour, finish, and seal. Fully scoped all-in price with permit.

missing_items: Demo and haul-away not mentioned — existing concrete removal may be excluded
red_flags: Demo exclusion risk — if not included, true total cost rises significantly and may exceed vendor-a
No warranty
Permit status unconfirmed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lower quoted price that likely excludes demo and haul-away — a significant cost that would close or reverse the apparent price gap with vendor-a.

description: Three fence installation quotes — one explicitly uses HOA-prohibited pressure-treated pine, one unspecified material, one confirmed cedar. Risk tolerance: Low with HOA material requirements.

input_data.assessment_notes: 220 linear feet of 6-foot privacy fence, wood, replacing existing rotted fence. HOA CC&Rs require cedar or redwood — pressure-treated pine is explicitly prohibited. Front corner posts need concrete footings per HOA. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: Western red cedar dog-ear panels, 220 LF
4x4 cedar posts, concrete footings at all corners and 8-ft intervals
Remove and haul existing fence

red_flags: No mention of HOA approval documentation service
evaluated_quotes.summary: Western red cedar fence matching HOA specifications with permit, concrete footings, and demo included. Clean scope.

sub_quotes.description: Privacy fence panels, posts, hardware
Tear down and dispose of existing fence

missing_items: Lumber species not specified — may not meet HOA cedar/redwood requirement
red_flags: Lumber species unspecified — HOA compliance unverified
No permit confirmation
evaluated_quotes.summary: Mid-range quote without confirmed lumber species. HOA compliance cannot be verified without clarification.

red_flags: Pressure-treated pine likely — explicitly prohibited by HOA CC&Rs
No demo included in price
No permit
If rejected by HOA, full removal and replacement at homeowner expense
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lowest bid but likely uses HOA-prohibited materials. Installation and subsequent forced removal would cost more than any other vendor.

description: Three shower tile replacement quotes — one includes waterproofing membrane, two do not. Tests whether model identifies the waterproofing gap as a long-term structural and liability risk.

input_data.assessment_notes: Master bath shower, full tile replacement, 60 sq ft floor and walls. Previous grout has failed in spots and homeowner suspects moisture behind tile. Wants a lasting result. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: Full tile demo, inspect backer board, replace any damaged substrate
Schluter KERDI membrane applied to all shower surfaces before tile
Porcelain tile, unsanded grout, silicone at all corners

evaluated_quotes.summary: Complete shower tile replacement with professional waterproofing membrane and substrate inspection. Most comprehensive quote.

sub_quotes.description: Remove existing tile, light surface prep
Tile installation on existing backer board, grout and seal

missing_items: No moisture or substrate inspection mentioned
red_flags: No waterproofing membrane — tile over failed substrate without proper moisture barrier risks repeat failure within 2-5 years
Existing moisture damage not addressed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Mid-range quote that installs over existing substrate without waterproofing. Given the noted moisture concerns, this approach risks repeating the same failure.

red_flags: No demo — tile may be installed over existing failed tile
No waterproofing or moisture assessment
Cannot determine actual scope from quote
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lowest bid with minimal detail. Unclear whether existing tile is even being removed. Highest risk of near-term failure given stated moisture concerns.

description: Two quotes for a whole-home window replacement (12 windows). One vendor bundles installation with a manufacturer warranty, the other installs third-party windows with no warranty coordination. Tests warranty-gap identification.

input_data.assessment_notes: Replace all 12 double-hung windows in a 1990s colonial. Current windows are fogged and showing seal failure. Homeowner wants windows that will last and has had warranty issues with contractors before. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: Andersen factory-direct windows, 20-year transferable manufacturer warranty
Professional installation, interior and exterior trim restoration
Remove and dispose all old windows, full job site cleanup

evaluated_quotes.summary: Authorized Andersen dealer installation with 20-year transferable warranty, permit, and full cleanup. Highest bid but comprehensive coverage.

missing_items: Window brand and model not specified
red_flags: Window brand unknown — quality and warranty unverifiable
No transferable warranty means coverage does not pass to future buyer
Permit status unconfirmed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lower bid with unspecified windows and no warranty detail. Given homeowner's prior warranty issues, the absence of warranty terms is a meaningful gap.

description: Four electrical quotes for a 3-unit property with insurance deadline. One vendor has partial scope on Unit 3 that may not satisfy insurer. Tests risk prioritization under time pressure.

input_data.assessment_notes: Three-unit residential property (duplex + basement unit). Full knob-and-tube wiring replacement required across all units per insurance carrier notice. Each unit needs panel upgrade and new circuit installation. Insurance policy renewal in 90 days — work must be completed and inspected by then. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: Full K&T replacement, 200A panel upgrade, permit included
Full K&T replacement, 200A panel upgrade, larger unit with finished basement
Full K&T replacement, 100A panel, basement unit

red_flags: No project timeline — insurance deadline at risk
Payment milestone schedule missing
evaluated_quotes.summary: Comprehensive K&T replacement across all three units with panel upgrades. Fully itemized by unit. Will pull permits. No timeline provided despite noted insurance deadline.

sub_quotes.description: K&T replacement for upper two units, panel upgrades included
Partial K&T replacement, panel upgrade — excludes finished wall areas

missing_items: Unit 3 scope explicitly excludes finished wall areas — incomplete remediation
red_flags: Unit 3 exclusion of finished wall areas may not satisfy insurance carrier requirements
Partial scope on Unit 3 could trigger policy rejection
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lower bid with full scope on Units 1 and 2 but explicit exclusion on Unit 3 finished wall areas. Insurance compliance risk on Unit 3.

sub_quotes.description: Complete K&T removal and replacement across all three units
200A panel upgrades for all three units, includes permits and inspection coordination

missing_items: No unit-by-unit breakdown — difficult to verify per-unit coverage for insurer
red_flags: No per-unit breakdown makes it harder to confirm full compliance per insurance requirements
evaluated_quotes.summary: Highest bid with comprehensive scope and explicit inspection coordination. Will pull permits. No per-unit breakdown.

missing_items: No warranty or inspection process described
red_flags: No permits — automatic failure for insurance compliance
Single lump sum with no scope detail
Significantly below market for 3-unit K&T replacement
Panel upgrades not confirmed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lowest bid by a wide margin. Single lump sum with no itemization, no permit commitment, and no panel upgrade confirmation. Highest risk for insurance compliance.

description: Five roofing quotes following hail damage. Insurance adjuster report specifies Class 4 shingles, synthetic underlayment, ice shield, and drip edge. Tests whether model flags quotes that fail to meet the insurance spec.

input_data.assessment_notes: 2,400 sq ft ranch, hail damage across full roof. Insurance adjuster report specifies: Class 4 impact-resistant shingles, synthetic underlayment, drip edge replacement, and ice and water shield at all eaves. Approved claim amount: $18,400. Any upgrade cost is homeowner responsibility. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: GAF Timberline ArmorShield II, Class 4, 30-year warranty
Synthetic underlayment, ice and water shield at all eaves, new drip edge
Full tearoff, haul-away, installation, cleanup

red_flags: $500 above claim amount — homeowner owes the difference
evaluated_quotes.summary: Fully insurance-compliant quote meeting all adjuster-specified materials. $500 over claim amount.

sub_quotes.description: Owens Corning Duration — impact resistance rating not specified
Felt underlayment, labor and tearoff

missing_items: Impact resistance rating not confirmed — Class 4 required
Synthetic underlayment not specified — felt used instead
Ice and water shield not mentioned
red_flags: Impact rating unconfirmed — insurance may reject claim payout
Felt underlayment does not meet adjuster spec
Ice and water shield absent
Drip edge not addressed
evaluated_quotes.summary: Within claim amount but fails to meet multiple adjuster-specified material requirements. Insurance non-payment risk.

missing_items: No mention of underlayment type, ice shield, or drip edge
red_flags: No material specs — likely does not meet insurance requirements
No permit
If insurance rejects, homeowner absorbs full cost
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lowest bid with unspecified materials likely falling short of insurance requirements. High risk of claim rejection.

sub_quotes.description: CertainTeed Landmark IR, Class 4, lifetime limited warranty
Synthetic underlayment, full ice and water shield (not just eaves), drip edge, ridge vent upgrade
Full tearoff, new plywood inspection, cleanup

red_flags: $5,100 above insurance claim — significant homeowner out-of-pocket for upgrades
evaluated_quotes.summary: Fully compliant plus upgrades including lifetime shingles and full ice shield beyond eaves-only. Highest bid with meaningful out-of-pocket over claim amount.

sub_quotes.description: GAF ArmorShield II Class 4 shingles, labor included
Synthetic underlayment and drip edge — ice and water shield not included

missing_items: Ice and water shield not included — required by adjuster
red_flags: Ice and water shield excluded — adjuster specification not fully met
Partial compliance may affect claim reimbursement
evaluated_quotes.summary: Mostly compliant but explicitly excludes the ice and water shield required by the insurance adjuster. Partial claim non-payment risk.

description: Three foundation repair quotes using different methods. Structural engineer report specifies helical piers. One vendor deviates on pier count, one uses an unsupported alternative method. Risk tolerance: Low with pending home sale.

input_data.assessment_notes: Residential home with 1.5-inch differential settlement at northwest corner. Independent structural engineer report recommends helical pier installation, minimum 6 piers, to reach load-bearing soil. Engineer will re-inspect post-repair. Homeowner is selling in 6 months and needs a transferable warranty for buyer's lender. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: 6 helical piers per engineer spec, driven to load-bearing soil, torque-verified
Hydraulic lift to specified tolerance, re-level per engineer plan
Coordinate with homeowner's structural engineer for post-repair sign-off

evaluated_quotes.summary: Matches engineer-specified method and pier count exactly. Includes permit, transferable warranty, and engineer coordination for post-repair sign-off. Highest bid but fully spec-compliant.

sub_quotes.description: 5 helical piers — contractor recommends 5 vs engineer-specified 6
Lift and level per their standard tolerance

missing_items: Only 5 piers vs 6 specified by engineer
red_flags: Fewer piers than engineer spec — deviation may void warranty or fail re-inspection
No engineer coordination — post-repair sign-off at risk, which could delay home sale
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lower bid using helical piers but deviates from engineer-specified pier count. Engineer re-inspection may fail, which would delay the home sale.

missing_items: Uses mudjacking — not the method specified by engineer
Transferable warranty not available for this method
red_flags: Mudjacking does not address root cause of differential settlement per engineer report
Method substitution without engineer approval invalidates the engineer's report
Will not satisfy buyer's lender or inspector at home sale
No permit
evaluated_quotes.summary: Significantly lower bid using a method not recommended by the structural engineer. Will not satisfy the engineer re-inspection requirement and likely cannot produce a transferable warranty.

description: Three bathroom remodel quotes in a pre-1978 home where EPA RRP lead-safe practices are legally required. Only one quote addresses it. Tests whether model treats regulatory non-compliance as a deal-breaker regardless of price.

input_data.assessment_notes: Full master bathroom remodel — tile replacement, new vanity, toilet, and shower door. Home built in 1962. Contractors will be disturbing existing walls and tile. EPA Renovation, Repair, and Painting (RRP) Rule requires lead-safe work practices when disturbing painted surfaces in pre-1978 homes. Risk tolerance: Low.

sub_quotes.description: EPA-certified RRP test prior to demo, lead-safe work practices and proper disposal if positive
Full tile removal under lead-safe containment if required
New tile, vanity, toilet, shower door, plumbing hookup

evaluated_quotes.summary: Only quote that addresses EPA RRP lead-safe requirements. Highest bid but includes legally required testing and safe handling practices for a pre-1978 home.

sub_quotes.description: Full tile demo, wall prep
Tile install, vanity, toilet, shower door

missing_items: No mention of lead paint assessment or RRP compliance
red_flags: No lead-safe protocol on pre-1978 home — federal EPA RRP rule violation risk
Contractor may not be EPA RRP certified — homeowner legal and health liability
evaluated_quotes.summary: Competitive mid-range quote with no acknowledgment of lead paint risk. EPA RRP compliance gap is a federal violation risk and a health risk for occupants.

red_flags: No lead-safe protocol on pre-1978 home
No permit
Lowest bid likely excludes required regulatory compliance costs entirely
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lowest bid with no permit and no lead paint protocol. Creates both federal legal exposure and health risk for homeowner and occupants in a pre-1978 home.

description: Only one vendor provided. Tests that the model does not fabricate a comparison or invent benchmark prices to fill the gap.

input_data.assessment_notes: Homeowner received only one quote for a bathroom remodel (tile replacement, vanity swap, toilet replacement). No prior quotes or budget established. Risk tolerance: High.

red_flags: No permit mentioned for potential plumbing work
No warranty terms
No project timeline
evaluated_quotes.summary: Single lump sum for full bathroom tile replacement, vanity replacement, and toilet swap. Minimal detail on materials or timeline.

description: Two quotes where assessment notes specify a hard move-in deadline, but neither quote includes a timeline. Tests whether model escalates the deadline risk as a deal-breaker, not a minor flag.

input_data.assessment_notes: Homeowner needs HVAC replacement completed before August 1 — tenant move-in date is fixed and lease is signed. Current date is June 15. Neither contractor has been asked about availability. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: Carrier 16 SEER split system, 10-year parts warranty
Full installation, permits, startup and testing

missing_items: No project timeline or start date provided
red_flags: No timeline provided — 46-day window to August 1 deadline is at risk if scheduling not confirmed immediately
evaluated_quotes.summary: Well-scoped HVAC replacement with solid warranty and permit, but no timeline despite a hard August 1 deadline in the assessment notes.

red_flags: No timeline — same August 1 deadline risk as vendor-a
Permit status unclear
Equipment and warranty unspecified
evaluated_quotes.summary: Lower-cost quote with significant data gaps including no timeline, unspecified equipment, and unclear permit status. Deadline risk compounded by missing scope detail.

description: One vendor's sub_quote line items sum to $28,400 but total_amount is listed as $24,000 — a $4,400 discrepancy. Tests whether model catches and flags the math error before recommending.

input_data.assessment_notes: Full kitchen remodel, 180 sq ft, gut renovation including new cabinets, counters, appliances, and flooring. Homeowner has a fixed budget of $45,000. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: Semi-custom cabinets, soft-close hinges, crown molding
Quartz countertops, undermount sink cutout
LVP installation with underlayment
Plumbing, electrical, appliance connections

missing_items: Discrepancy between sub_quote total ($28,400) and stated total_amount ($24,000) — requires clarification before signing
red_flags: Sub_quote line items sum to $28,400 but total_amount is listed as $24,000 — $4,400 unexplained discrepancy that must be resolved before contract execution
evaluated_quotes.summary: Detailed kitchen remodel quote with a pricing discrepancy between line items and total that must be clarified before this quote can be relied upon.

sub_quotes.description: Full custom cabinets with pull-out shelves
Quartzite slab countertops
Hardwood floor installation
Plumbing, electrical, project management

red_flags: No project timeline
evaluated_quotes.summary: Premium full-service kitchen remodel with custom cabinets and natural stone. Exceeds stated $45K budget.

description: High risk tolerance homeowner, but one quote involves unpermitted electrical panel work — illegal in all U.S. jurisdictions. Tests that model escalates absolute legal deal-breakers regardless of stated risk tolerance.

input_data.assessment_notes: 200A electrical panel upgrade and whole-home rewire, 1,400 sq ft. Homeowner wants the cheapest option and is explicitly willing to accept risk to save money. Risk tolerance: High.

sub_quotes.description: 200A panel, permit, utility notification, inspection
Full rewire to code, all circuits labeled, GFCI/AFCI where required

evaluated_quotes.summary: Code-compliant full rewire and panel upgrade. Licensed contractor with permit and inspection included.

red_flags: Electrical panel work without permit is illegal in all U.S. jurisdictions — homeowner insurance is voided in the event of fire
Unlicensed electrical work creates irreversible safety and resale risk
No inspection means no recourse if work fails
Price 51% below licensed quote strongly suggests unlicensed contractor
evaluated_quotes.summary: Significantly below-market quote with no permit and no confirmed licensing. Electrical work without a permit creates legal, safety, and insurance void exposure that risk tolerance cannot offset.

description: Three quotes with identical total prices but completely different scope. No price-based differentiation. Tests whether model recommends based on scope completeness and risk alone.

input_data.assessment_notes: Replace 18-year-old HVAC system in a 1,900 sq ft two-story home. All three vendors were given a fixed price target of $10,500. Homeowner wants best value within that number. Risk tolerance: Medium.

sub_quotes.description: 16 SEER split system, 10-year manufacturer warranty, smart thermostat included
Full installation, duct inspection, system commissioning
Permit, 2-year labor warranty, 24/7 service line

evaluated_quotes.summary: Full-scope HVAC replacement at the agreed price. Best equipment tier, permit, smart thermostat, and 2-year labor warranty.

sub_quotes.description: 14 SEER unit, standard thermostat, installation
Permit pull only

red_flags: 14 SEER is minimum code — no efficiency advantage
No labor warranty — post-install repair costs uncovered
evaluated_quotes.summary: Same price as vendor-a but lower SEER rating and no labor warranty. Meets minimum requirements but delivers less long-term value.

missing_items: No duct work or inspection mentioned
red_flags: Equipment specs unknown at stated price — value cannot be verified
Permit unclear
No warranty of any kind
evaluated_quotes.summary: Same price but no scope detail provided. Cannot verify what equipment or services are included. Highest opacity at the same price point.
